name: Steamed Apple-Date Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. Heavily grease a deep 2 1/2-quart heat-safe bowl. Cut foil slightly larger than the top of the bowl to use as cover. Grease one side of the foil well; set aside.
2. In a blender at medium speed or in a food processor with the knife blade attached, blend dates and apple juice until smooth; set aside.
3. In a large bowl, with a mixer on low speed, beat sugar and butter or margarine just until blended. Increase speed to high; beat until light and fluffy. Add eggs and the next five ingredients, beating at low speed until blended. Increase speed to medium; beat 1 minute, occasionally scraping the bowl with a rubber spatula. With a rubber spatula, gently fold in chopped apple and walnuts. Spoon the pudding mixture into the prepared bowl. Cover the bowl with foil, greased side down, and tie tightly with a string.
4. Set the bowl on a rack in an 8-12 quart saucepan. Pour water into the saucepan so it comes halfway up the side of the bowl but does not touch the foil. Heat over low heat until boiling. Reduce heat to low; cover and simmer for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until a skewer inserted through the foil into the center of the pudding comes out clean.
5. About 30 minutes before the pudding is done, prepare the Custard Sauce.
6. When the pudding is done, let it cool in the bowl on a wire rack for 10 minutes. Loosen the pudding with a metal spatula and invert onto a warm platter. Garnish with red and green candied cherries. Serve the pudding warm with the warm sauce.
Custard Sauce:
1. In a heavy 2-quart saucepan over low heat, or in a double boiler over hot (not boiling) water, beat egg yolks, sugar, and salt with a wire whisk. Gradually stir in milk while cooking, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and coats the back of a spoon, about 25 minutes. Stir in vanilla extract. Serve warm or cold over apple pie, fruitcake, cut-up fruit, or ice cream.
